faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Pomonok apartment?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, webbing in closet corners, adult moths flying near wardrobes (especially at dusk), and larvae or cocoons in fabric folds. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, rugs, or stored blankets. If you spot any of these signs in your Pomonok home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Pomonok?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Pomonok typically range from $550 to $5,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your living space, and the number of treatments required. Co-op apartments generally cost less than larger single-family homes. We provide free estimates and will assess your specific situation to give you an accurate quote before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in my Pomonok home?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved, low-toxicity treatments that are safe for children, pets, and sensitive individuals when applied correctly. Our integrated pest management approach prioritizes non-chemical methods like vacuuming, heat treatment, and pheromone traps whenever possible. We'll discuss all treatment options with you and ensure you understand any necessary precautions for your specific Pomonok residence.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Pomonok apartment after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning closets and storage areas, storing woolens and natural fibers in sealed containers or garment bags, dry cleaning items before long-term storage, and maintaining good ventilation. We also recommend periodic inspections, cedar products as natural repellents, and freezing suspect items for 72 hours. Our technicians will provide customized prevention tips based on your Pomonok home's specific layout and risk factors.
